definition: A compound analogue framework using multi-dimensional scoring is methodologically sounder than reliance on any single historical episode. No episode scores above 7.0/10 similarity due to four structural novelties: mortgage lock-in (85%+ below 5%), >90% covenant-lite, AI investment cycle, and simultaneous deglobalization + fiscal expansion at 6-7% GDP.
evidence: pair_0 judged compound approach stronger than single-analogue reliance
Four structural novelties limit any single analogue to <7.0/10 similarity
Seven-dimension scoring methodology provides systematic comparison
open_questions: Are the dimension weights in the scoring framework objective or subjective?
At what point does compound structural novelty invalidate analogue-based reasoning entirely?
Are outcome distributions wider than any analogue framework suggests?
